title: Seele Monoliths
wikitext:
[]: Seele Monoliths             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Jan 04, 2013 3:56 pm In the T.V. series of Evangelion, the Seele monoliths were pretty nondescript. They simply had the number and mode of conversation. However, something changes about them in RoE. Specifically, on the back of the monoliths. Whereas before they were solid black, they now have images. One was of particular interest to me: a representation of the Pythagorean theorem. Why is this symbol on the back of the Seele monoliths? One popular idea about the symbol in general is that it should be made visible from outer space to signify intelligent life on earth. This theory makes no sense as pertaining to the Evaverse, so what is it doing on Seele's backs?

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Fri Jan 04, 2013 4:05 pm What? I never even noticed the Seele monoliths got tattoos. Where exactly did you see it?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Jan 04, 2013 4:07 pm A.T. Fish wrote: What? I never even noticed the Seele monoliths got tattoos. Where exactly did you see it?

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Fri Jan 04, 2013 4:17 pm That's fine, I just saw it at about 23 minites in but apparently they all have the same symbol on their backs.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Jan 04, 2013 4:19 pm A.T. Fish wrote: That's fine, I just saw it at about 23 minutes in but apparently they all have the same symbol on their backs.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Fri Jan 04, 2013 4:24 pm Who knows? Knowing Eva it's very likely we'll never get an answer.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Jan 04, 2013 4:28 pm A.T. Fish wrote: Who knows? Knowing Eva it's very likely we'll never get an answer.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Jan 04, 2013 4:34 pm The Pythagoreans were known for the notion that numbers represent the true nature of things, and for purification rites with the intent of facilitating the transmigration of the soul to join a higher being. They also believed that the soul is limited by the physical form, which creates a conflict between the needs of the body and the true divinity of the disembodied soul.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jan 04, 2013 4:51 pm Which sounds a lot like most of their mythology babble about rites.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jan 04, 2013 6:39 pm Was originally brought up by Zuggy, he's since deleted his posts about it but I brought it back up here: http://forum.evageeks.org/post/190942/Seele-monoliths-reverse-sign/#190942 The free mason's link is too old so use archive.org: http://web.archive.org/web/20081221202058/http://masonicdictionary.com/47.html You can probably find a bunch of other random things about it if you search the forums for "Free mason"

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Jan 04, 2013 7:09 pm Chuckman, that is perfect.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Jan 04, 2013 7:31 pm Ornette wrote: Was originally brought up by Zuggy, he's since deleted his posts about it but I brought it back up here: http://forum.evageeks.org/post/190942/Seele-monoliths-reverse-sign/#190942 The free mason's link is too old so use archive.org: http://web.archive.org/web/20081221202058/http://masonicdictionary.com/47.html You can probably find a bunch of other random things about it if you search the forums for "Free mason"

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Jan 04, 2013 8:07 pm Can someone post a good image of the symbol here? All of the relevant images in that thread that Ornette linked have been removed.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jan 04, 2013 8:35 pm So basically Seele IS the Illuminati. Bob won't be liking this...

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jan 04, 2013 9:05 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: So basically Seele IS the Illuminati. Bob won't be liking this...

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jan 04, 2013 9:08 pm Bob BQ. He didn't like it when the CI basically said Seele was the Illuminati because it was lazy and uninspired.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Jan 04, 2013 9:43 pm They're nothing at all like the Bavarian Illuiminati. The fictional Illuminati are just short hand for "Big Bad Background Conspiracy" so they do fit that mold. Seele are pretty much a western esoteric order, down to the way they're organized and the symbols and language they use.

[]:             Quote Post by DeadClones » Sat Jan 05, 2013 2:14 am Sorry about the poor quality, I was rewatching it on some unreputable website to see the symbols in question. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Jan 13, 2013 2:45 am In 3.0, SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Jan 13, 2013 8:10 pm Nice Find!

Article: Seele Monoliths
[]: Seele Monoliths             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Jan 04, 2013 3:56 pm In the T.V. series of Evangelion, the Seele monoliths were pretty nondescript. They simply had the number and mode of conversation. However, something changes about them in RoE. Specifically, on the back of the monoliths. Whereas before they were solid black, they now have images. One was of particular interest to me: a representation of the Pythagorean theorem. Why is this symbol on the back of the Seele monoliths? One popular idea about the symbol in general is that it should be made visible from outer space to signify intelligent life on earth. This theory makes no sense as pertaining to the Evaverse, so what is it doing on Seele's backs?
